old_claim.note: X-Ray Imaging provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Medical ultrasound requires ultrasonic transduction to generate and receive sound pulses, not ionizing X-ray imaging.

source_shape.source_locator: IPEM history describing Langevin's piezoelectric ultrasonic transducers and their role in modern ultrasound.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source describes piezoelectric ultrasonic transducers as the core technology behind modern ultrasound and traces their development from Langevin's sonar work.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports the required transducer edge because ultrasound imaging needs sound generation and echo detection, while X-ray imaging is a different ionizing-radiation modality.

ontology_before: Ultrasound medical imaging depended on X-ray imaging, conflating neighboring radiology modalities.
ontology_after: Ultrasound now depends on ultrasonic transducers and clinical/electronic foundations, keeping it distinct from X-ray imaging.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: ultrasound is no longer downstream of X-rays. Preserved: it remains in the medical imaging field with source-backed modality foundations.
would_reject_if: A source showed diagnostic ultrasound physically requires X-ray imaging as a prerequisite.
The graph reintroduced x_ray_imaging as a direct ultrasound prerequisite.
The transducer node were broadened so much that it no longer represented ultrasonic pulse generation and echo detection.
short_reason: Ultrasound is a sound-transducer modality, not an X-ray modality.
